title: The AI Revolution: How Artificial Intelligence is Changing the World of Work
subtitle: As companies adopt new technology, workers face a future of job cuts, team reshuffles, and new ways of working.

summary.en: Major companies are increasingly using artificial intelligence to improve efficiency and reduce costs. This shift has led to significant job losses at large firms like Standard Chartered and Meta. Employees are experiencing rapid changes, including forced team transfers and new workplace monitoring tools. While some experts see a path to higher productivity, many young people fear that AI will cause serious economic and social problems. Universities and businesses are struggling to prepare the next generation for this new, technology-driven job market.
summary.zh: 各大企業正日益運用人工智慧來提升效率並降低成本。這種轉變已導致渣打銀行與 Meta 等大型企業出現大規模裁員。員工正經歷劇烈變動，包括被迫調職以及面臨新的職場監控工具。儘管部分專家認為這能通往更高的生產力，但許多年輕人擔心人工智慧將引發嚴重的經濟與社會問題。大學與企業正努力協助下一代為這個由科技驅動的新就業市場做好準備。

paragraphs.en: The global workforce is facing a period of rapid change as companies race to adopt artificial intelligence (AI). AI refers to computer systems that can perform tasks usually requiring human intelligence, such as making decisions or analyzing data. While many businesses hope that these tools will make them more productive, the impact on employees is becoming increasingly clear and, for many, quite difficult.
paragraphs.zh: 全球勞動力正經歷一段快速變遷的時期，各家企業競相採用人工智慧（AI）。人工智慧指的是能執行通常需要人類智慧才能完成之任務的電腦系統，例如決策或分析數據。雖然許多企業希望這些工具能提升生產力，但其對員工的影響正變得日益顯著，對許多人而言，情況也相當艱難。

paragraphs.en: Standard Chartered, a major bank based in London, recently announced plans to reduce its workforce. According to the BBC, the bank intends to cut about 7,800 back-office roles by 2030. This represents 15% of its staff in those areas. The bank’s chief executive, Bill Winters, explained that the move is part of a strategy to use automation and AI to improve efficiency and decision-making. He noted that the bank is replacing lower-value human work with technology investments. While Standard Chartered hopes to move some affected workers to other parts of the business, the trend of using AI to streamline operations is spreading across the financial sector. Research from Morgan Stanley previously estimated that AI could put over 200,000 European banking jobs at risk by 2030.
paragraphs.zh: 總部位於倫敦的渣打銀行（Standard Chartered）近期宣布了縮編計畫。根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，該銀行打算在二〇三〇年前裁減約七千八百個後勤職位，這佔了該領域員工總數的百分之十五。銀行執行長溫特斯（Bill Winters）解釋，此舉是利用自動化與人工智慧來提升效率與決策品質策略的一部分。他指出，銀行正以科技投資取代價值較低的勞力工作。儘管渣打銀行希望將部分受影響的員工轉調至業務的其他部門，但利用人工智慧精簡營運的趨勢正蔓延至整個金融業。摩根士丹利（Morgan Stanley）先前的研究估計，到二〇三〇年，人工智慧可能使歐洲銀行業超過二十萬個職位面臨風險。

paragraphs.en: Technology companies are also undergoing massive changes. Meta, the owner of Facebook, is currently reorganizing its staff to focus heavily on AI. The Guardian reports that thousands of employees have been moved to new teams, such as those building AI cloud infrastructure. In some cases, these transfers are not optional. One Meta engineer told The Guardian that the company’s new management style feels like a command-and-order system, which has caused unhappiness among staff. Furthermore, Meta has introduced a tool called the Model Capability Initiative (MCI) to monitor workers. This tool tracks mouse movements, keystrokes, and other activities to provide data for training AI models. A spokesperson for Meta stated that this data is necessary to help AI agents learn how to perform everyday computer tasks, and that there are safeguards to protect sensitive information.
paragraphs.zh: 科技公司也正在經歷巨大的變革。臉書（Facebook）的母公司 Meta 目前正在重組人力，以高度聚焦於人工智慧。衛報（The Guardian）報導，數千名員工已被調往新團隊，例如負責建構人工智慧雲端基礎設施的部門。在某些情況下，這些調動並非出於自願。一名 Meta 工程師告訴衛報，公司新的管理風格感覺像是命令式系統，這已引發員工不滿。此外，Meta 引進了一款名為「模型能力倡議」（Model Capability Initiative, MCI）的工具來監控員工。該工具會追蹤滑鼠移動、按鍵輸入及其他活動，以提供訓練人工智慧模型的數據。Meta 發言人表示，這些數據對於協助人工智慧代理程式學習如何執行日常電腦任務是必要的，且公司設有保護機制以維護敏感資訊的安全。

paragraphs.en: These changes are happening during a time of great uncertainty for many workers. Large tech firms like Amazon and Oracle have also announced significant layoffs in recent months, as they spend more money on building AI infrastructure. For many employees, the promise of AI is overshadowed by the fear of losing their jobs or having their daily work habits monitored by the very systems they are helping to build.
paragraphs.zh: 這些變革發生在許多勞工感到極度不安的時期。亞馬遜（Amazon）與甲骨文（Oracle）等大型科技公司近幾個月也宣布了大規模裁員，因為他們將更多資金投入於建構人工智慧基礎設施。對許多員工而言，人工智慧帶來的願景，已被擔心失去工作，或是擔心日常工作習慣受到他們親手協助開發的系統所監控的恐懼所掩蓋。

paragraphs.en: This fear is particularly strong among young people. A recent study by King’s College London (KCL) found that one in three university students believes that AI will destroy jobs so quickly that it will cause civil unrest—a situation where large groups of people protest or cause disorder due to unhappiness with society. The poll showed that while students are frequent users of AI, they are also very worried about its economic impact. Bobby Duffy, the director of the KCL Policy Institute, noted that the public and young people are watching the development of AI with more fear than excitement. He emphasized that there is real concern regarding the future of entry-level jobs.
paragraphs.zh: 這種恐懼在年輕人之間尤為強烈。倫敦國王學院（KCL）近期的一項研究發現，三分之一的大學生認為人工智慧將導致工作機會迅速消失，進而引發社會動盪——即大批民眾因對社會不滿而發起抗議或造成混亂的情況。民調顯示，雖然學生是人工智慧的頻繁使用者，但他們也對其經濟影響感到非常擔憂。倫敦國王學院政策研究所所長達菲（Bobby Duffy）指出，大眾與年輕人看待人工智慧發展的態度，恐懼多過於興奮。他強調，對於入門級職位的未來，確實存在真實的憂慮。

paragraphs.en: Despite these fears, some experts believe the future does not have to be negative. Bouke Klein Teeselink, a lecturer at KCL, suggested that with the right training and government policies, AI could lead to a better future. He argued that it could create broader opportunities, higher incomes, and faster scientific progress. However, there is currently a gap between what students need and what they are receiving. While 60% of students believe universities should prepare them for an AI-shaped job market, only 36% feel that they are actually getting that preparation.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管存在這些恐懼，一些專家認為未來未必是負面的。倫敦國王學院講師提斯林克（Bouke Klein Teeselink）認為，透過正確的培訓與政府政策，人工智慧可能引領出更好的未來。他主張這能創造更廣泛的機會、更高的收入以及更快速的科學進步。然而，目前學生所需與實際獲得的資源之間存在落差。雖然百分之六十的學生認為大學應協助他們為人工智慧主導的就業市場做好準備，但僅有百分之三十六的學生認為自己確實獲得了相關準備。

paragraphs.en: As the world continues to integrate AI into daily business, the debate over its impact will likely grow. For now, companies are focused on using technology to increase profitability and stay ahead of competitors. Meanwhile, workers and students are left to navigate a changing landscape where the skills they learn today may need to be updated tomorrow. Whether AI will be a tool for human progress or a cause for social instability remains one of the most important questions of our time.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著全球持續將人工智慧整合至日常商業活動中，關於其影響的辯論可能會持續升溫。目前，企業專注於利用科技來提升獲利並保持競爭優勢。與此同時，勞工與學生只能在不斷變化的環境中摸索，今日所學的技能明日可能就需要更新。人工智慧究竟會成為人類進步的工具，還是社會不穩定的導火線，仍是我們這個時代最重要的議題之一。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To compete or move very quickly to achieve something before others.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（比賽），這裡作動詞，強調企業為了競爭而加速行動。文中：The global workforce is facing a period of rapid change as companies race to adopt artificial intelligence (AI).

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make something seem less important or less happy than it should be.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 使……顯得黯然失色、蒙上陰影。
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意是「遮蔽」，這裡用來形容恐懼感蓋過了原本對科技的正面期待。文中：For many employees, the promise of AI is overshadowed by the fear of losing their jobs or having their daily work habits monitored by the very systems they are helping to build.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To deal with a difficult or complex situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意是「導航」，這裡指在變動的職場環境中設法生存與適應。文中：Meanwhile, workers and students are left to navigate a changing landscape where the skills they learn today may need to be updated tomorrow.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Standard Chartered to cut thousands of roles as AI use increases

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Meta is rapidly reorganizing its workers’ jobs around AI: ‘Transfers aren’t optional’

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Standard Chartered to cut more than 7,000 jobs as it steps up AI use

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Third of university students in Great Britain think AI job losses will cause social unrest, poll finds

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the primary purpose of Meta's 'Model Capability Initiative' (MCI)?

options.A: To monitor the productivity levels of engineers to determine salary bonuses.
options.B: To collect data on human computer usage to train AI models.
options.C: To enforce a new command-and-order management style across the company.
options.D: To protect sensitive information from being accessed by unauthorized AI agents.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，Meta 使用 MCI 工具來追蹤滑鼠移動、按鍵等活動，目的是為了提供數據來訓練 AI 模型（provide data for training AI models）。選項 A 錯誤，文章未提及與薪資獎金有關；選項 C 錯誤，雖然員工提到公司管理風格像命令式系統，但 MCI 本身是監控工具而非管理風格的定義；選項 D 錯誤，文章提到有安全防護措施保護資訊，但這不是 MCI 的主要目的。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might university students feel a sense of 'civil unrest' regarding AI?

options.A: They believe that AI will eventually replace all university professors.
options.B: They are frustrated that universities are not teaching them how to use AI tools.
options.C: They fear that rapid job displacement will lead to widespread social dissatisfaction.
options.D: They are concerned that AI will make it impossible for them to graduate on time.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。第五段提到學生擔心 AI 摧毀工作的速度太快，導致「社會動盪」（civil unrest），並解釋這指的是因為對社會不滿而引發的大規模抗議或混亂。選項 A 與 D 在文中皆未提及；選項 B 雖然提到學生認為學校準備不足，但這並非導致「社會動盪」的主要推論，學生對經濟影響的恐懼才是核心原因。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'landscape' mean in the context of 'a changing landscape'?

options.A: A physical area of land that is being modified for new office buildings.
options.B: A set of circumstances or a situation that is currently evolving.
options.C: The visual appearance of a workplace after AI has been integrated.
options.D: A collection of new skills that workers are required to learn by tomorrow.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此處 'landscape' 並非指地理景觀，而是比喻職場環境或整體局勢（a set of circumstances or a situation）。選項 A 過於字面解釋，與文章主題不符；選項 C 雖然在談論職場，但 landscape 在此是指抽象的環境變化而非視覺外觀；選項 D 錯誤，landscape 指的是環境本身，而非技能集合。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the rise of AI in the workforce?

options.A: AI is primarily a tool for scientific progress that will eventually eliminate the need for human labor.
options.B: Companies are successfully using AI to make employees happier and more productive.
options.C: The rapid adoption of AI creates a complex tension between corporate efficiency and worker security.
options.D: Governments must immediately ban AI in the financial sector to prevent economic collapse.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文探討了企業為了效率與利潤積極導入 AI，但這卻導致了員工對失業、監控及未來不確定性的恐懼，顯示出企業需求與勞工權益之間的張力。選項 A 過度悲觀且未涵蓋企業觀點；選項 B 與文章提到員工感到困難與恐懼的事實相反；選項 D 錯誤，文中僅提到需要政策支持，並未主張全面禁止 AI。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
